name: Hokkaido Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the dark blue of the seas around Hokkaido
snow and the brightness of the prefecture's future
passion, vitality and the rising sun

description: A dark navy-blue field charged at the centre with a white seven-pointed star, within which is a smaller red five-pointed star.
history: Hokkaido's prefectural symbol was selected in a public competition and adopted on 1 May 1967 to mark the centenary of the Meiji restoration and the prefecture's modern administration. The flag, with the symbol on a dark blue field, has been in use since the same date. The seven-pointed star is sometimes called the 'North Star of Hokkaido' (Hokushin).

features.symbolism: the deep blue of the northern sea surrounding Hokkaido

a stylised seven-pointed star (the 'Hokkaido star' or hokushin, North Star) representing the bright future of the northernmost prefecture; the seven points are also read as the seven sub-prefectures (shicho).

features.role: central red star of the emblem
features.symbolism: the rising sun and the warmth and vitality of the people of Hokkaido
